preamble: The shift from the voiced dental fricative [ð] to the glottal fricative [h] is a form of debuccalization. It represents an extreme stage of lenition where the oral dental constriction is abandoned, leaving only the glottal friction. This is documented as a transitional stage in heavily leniting Romance dialects.

examples.note: Intervocalic lenition where the dental fricative weakens to a glottal aspiration.
